assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: Classification is 'code change request' and Action is 'Sub-task created to address this feedback.' report.md Review Comment Classifications table: comment 30001 classified as 'Code change request' with Action 'Sub-task created'. subtask-1.md contains the corresponding sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as a code change request (or upgraded suggestion) and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: Classification is 'code change request' and Action is 'Sub-task created to address this feedback.' report.md Review Comment Classifications table: comment 30002 classified as 'Code change request' with Action 'Sub-task created'. subtask-2.md contains the corresponding sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: Classification is 'nit' and Action is 'No sub-task created.' report.md Review Comment Classifications table: comment 30003 classified as 'Nit' with Action 'No sub-task'. No subtask file references comment 30003.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: Classification is 'question' and Action is 'No sub-task created.' report.md Review Comment Classifications table: comment 30004 classified as 'Question' with Action 'No sub-task'. No subtask file references comment 30004.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md contains sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 21). subtask-2.md contains sections: '## Repository' (line 1), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 25). Both sub-tasks for review comments (subtask-1 and subtask-2) contain all required sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md line 36: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. subtask-2.md line 41: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 29-33: '## Review Context' section with PR Comment ID 30001, reviewer-a, file reference, and full comment text about wrapping UPDATE statements in a transaction. subtask-2.md lines 35-39: '## Review Context' section with PR Comment ID 30002, reviewer-a, file reference, and full comment text about adding an index on deleted_at.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), sub-task descriptions (subtask-1.md through subtask-4.md), and the report (report.md). No source code files are present in the outputs directory. The subtask files describe what should be modified but do not contain actual code changes. subtask-3.md and subtask-4.md are root-cause tasks for the sdlc-plugins repo and CONVENTIONS.md respectively, also descriptive only.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments are processed: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, and review-30004.md exist with full classification reasoning. The report.md Review Comment Classifications table lists all four comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) with their classifications and actions.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or merge actions. The report's overall result is 'FAIL' and it identifies outstanding sub-tasks. The report only describes verification findings and sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test files are new additions |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies comment 30002 directly as 'code change request' based on imperative language ('should also add') without discussing convention upgrade eligibility. The report's Root-Cause Investigation section mentions 'Convention gap (repo-specific pattern not documented)' and 'No documented convention in CONVENTIONS.md for adding indexes' but this is root-cause analysis, not convention upgrade eligibility evaluation. Neither review-30002.md nor the report contains a Style/Conventions analysis section that explicitly evaluates whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention for upgrade purposes.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classifies comment 30002 as 'code change request' with Action 'Sub-task created to address this feedback.' subtask-2.md contains the full sub-task description for adding the partial index. report.md confirms in the Review Comment Classifications table: comment 30002 has Action 'Sub-task created'.
